div1.entryFree:
Eye, vb. 1) to see: when first your eye I --d, Sonn. 104, 2. capering to e. her, Tp. V, 238. no man their works must e. Wiv. V, 5, 52. wild geese that the creeping fowler e. Mids. III, 2, 20. Mids. III, 2, 20 perspectives . . . --d awry distinguish form, R2 II, 2, 19. clambering the walls to e. him, Cor. II, 1, 226. saving those that e. thee, V, 3, 75. he --s us not, Cymb. V, 5, 124.
2) to look on, to observe, to hold in view: many a lady I have --d with best regard, Tp. III, 1, 40. e. your master's heels, Wiv. III, 2, 4. I --d them even to their ships, Wint. II, 1, 35. when she coldly --s the youthful Phoebus, Troil. I, 3, 229. my proceedings e. V, 7, 7. wherefore --st him so? Cymb. V, 5, 114.
3) intr. to look, appear: my becomings kill me, when they do not e. well to you, Ant. I, 3, 97.
